encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual encoder.h(3)

NAME

encoder.h - HEVC/H.265 encoder for ASCII art frames using libx265.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01
#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Functions


h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)
void h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t *ascii_data, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

HEVC/H.265 encoder for ASCII art frames using libx265.

Encodes terminal ASCII grid data using x265 for efficient compression. Handles variable-sized frames by storing dimensions in the encoded packet.

FRAME SIZE CHANGES: When terminal resize occurs (width/height change), the encoder must be reconfigured. This is handled transparently - the encoder detects size changes and reinitializes as needed.

PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][encoded_data: x265]

  • flags: Encoding flags (keyframe, etc.)
  • width: Frame width in characters
  • height: Frame height in characters
  • encoded_data: x265-encoded frame data

Definition in file encoder.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01

Encoder flags

Definition at line 33 of file encoder.h.

Referenced by h265_encode(), and h265_encoder_flush().

#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Definition at line 34 of file encoder.h.

Referenced by h265_encode().

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t * ascii_data, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Encode an ASCII art frame

The encoder automatically detects size changes and reinitializes when needed. The output packet includes frame dimensions so decoder can reconfigure.

Parameters

encoder Encoder handle
width Current frame width (characters)
height Current frame height (characters)
ascii_data Raw ASCII character grid (width × height bytes)
output_buf Buffer for encoded output
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

OUTPUT PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...]

Definition at line 91 of file comprehensive.c.

91                                                                                                                                                        {
92   (void)encoder;
93   (void)width;
94   (void)height;
95   (void)pixel_data;
96   (void)out_data;
97   (void)out_len;
98   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED, "H.265 encoding not supported in WASM");
99 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED, H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE, log_debug, log_dev,
    log_error, log_info, and SET_ERRNO.

    h265_client_capture_and_encode(), and
    threaded_send_image_frame_h265().






h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t
  initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)


Create a new x265 encoder for ASCII frames




Parameters


initial_width Initial frame width (characters)


initial_height Initial frame height (characters)


Returns


Encoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 54 of file encoder.c.


54                                                                                      {
55   if (initial_width == 0 || initial_height == 0) {
56     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
57     return NULL;
58   }
59 
60   h265_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_encoder_t), h265_encoder_t *);
61   if (!enc) {
62     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate encoder structure");
63     return NULL;
64   }
65 
66   // Find best available HEVC encoder (try by ID first, then by name for specific implementations)
67   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
68 
69   // If generic HEVC encoder not found, try specific implementations by name
70   if (!codec) {
71     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("libx265");
72   }
73   if (!codec) {
74     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_vaapi");
75   }
76   if (!codec) {
77     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_videotoolbox");
78   }
79   if (!codec) {
80     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_nvenc");
81   }
82   if (!codec) {
83     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_qsv");
84   }
85 
86   if (!codec) {
87     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC encoder not found");
88     SAFE_FREE(enc);
89     return NULL;
90   }
91 
92   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
93   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
94     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
95     SAFE_FREE(enc);
96     return NULL;
97   }
98 
99   // Configure codec context
100   enc->codec_ctx->width = initial_width;
101   enc->codec_ctx->height = initial_height;
102   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
103   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, 30};
104   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){30, 1};
105 
106   // Set ultrafast preset for low-latency encoding
107   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "preset", "ultrafast", 0) < 0) {
108     log_warn("Failed to set preset to ultrafast, using default");
109   }
110   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "tune", "zerolatency", 0) < 0) {
111     log_warn("Failed to set tune to zerolatency, using default");
112   }
113 
114   enc->codec_ctx->thread_count = h265_thread_count();
115 
116   // Defer avcodec_open2 to first encode call. The initial resolution (800x600) is
117   // usually wrong — the actual frame resolution is only known when the first capture
118   // arrives. Opening the codec here would create an x265 thread pool (~1s) that gets
119   // immediately discarded on reconfigure, wasting startup time.
120   enc->codec_opened = false;
121 
122   // Allocate frame and packet
123   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
124   enc->packet = av_packet_alloc();
125 
126   if (!enc->frame || !enc->packet) {
127     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
128     if (enc->frame)
129       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
130     if (enc->packet)
131       av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
132     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
133     SAFE_FREE(enc);
134     return NULL;
135   }
136 
137   enc->current_width = initial_width;
138   enc->current_height = initial_height;
139 
140   // Allocate YUV420P buffer (Y plane + U/V planes)
141   size_t stride = initial_width;
142   enc->yuv_buf_size = stride * initial_height * 3 / 2;
143   enc->yuv_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(enc->yuv_buf_size, uint8_t *);
144   if (!enc->yuv_buf) {
145     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate YUV buffer");
146     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
147     av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
148     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
149     SAFE_FREE(enc);
150     return NULL;
151   }
152 
153   log_info("FFmpeg HEVC encoder created: %ux%u (codec: %s)", initial_width, initial_height, codec->name);
154 
155   // Register encoder for debugging
156   NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER(enc, "h265_encoder", NULL);
157 
158   return enc;
159 }






References ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Destroy an x265 encoder




Parameters


encoder Encoder to destroy (may be NULL)

References LOG_IO, and SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by h265_client_context_create(), and
    h265_client_context_destroy().






asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush
  (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t *
  output_size)


Flush remaining buffered frames from encoder




Sends a NULL frame to the encoder to flush all buffered frames.
    This should be called before destroying the encoder or when you need to
    ensure all encoded data is retrieved.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


output_buf Buffer for encoded output


output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ASCIICHAT_OK with
  size=0 when no more data)


USAGE: // After final h265_encode() call, flush remaining
    frames while (size > 0) { size = max_size; h265_encoder_flush(encoder,
    buf, &size); if (size > 0) { // Send the flushed frame... } }


Definition at line 105 of file comprehensive.c.


105                                                                                                   {
106   (void)encoder;
107   (void)out_data;
108   (void)out_len;
109   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
110 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, log_debug, log_dev,
    log_error, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by protocol_stop_connection().






void
  h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint64_t *
  total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint32_t *
  avg_bitrate)


Get encoder statistics




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


total_frames Output: total frames encoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes encoded


avg_bitrate Output: average bitrate in bits per second


Definition at line 466 of file encoder.c.


467                                                    {
468   if (!encoder)
469     return;
470 
471   if (total_frames)
472     *total_frames = encoder->total_frames;
473   if (keyframes)
474     *keyframes = encoder->keyframes;
475   if (avg_bitrate)
476     *avg_bitrate = encoder->avg_bitrate;
477 }






Referenced by h265_client_get_stats().






void
  h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Request a keyframe on the next encode




Forces the encoder to produce an I-frame (keyframe) on the next
    call to h265_encoder_encode(). Useful for error recovery or stream
    synchronization.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


Definition at line 112 of file comprehensive.c.


112                                                             {
113   (void)encoder;
114 }






Referenced by h265_client_request_keyframe().
